ApiKeyResponse.description: Returned once when an API key is generated.

message.default: Store this key securely — it cannot be retrieved again.
message.title: Message

ApiKeyResponse.title: ApiKeyResponse

AssetCurrentLocation.description:
One row of the ``asset_current_location`` SQL view.

The latest known position for an active asset binding, badged by source
(`rfid` for the latest tag-read or one of the ``external_locations.source``
strings — e.g. `samsara`, `geotab`, `manual` — for the latest external
fix). Whichever side is newer wins.
AssetCurrentLocation.title: AssetCurrentLocation

AssetInZoneSummary.description: One row of `GET /zones/{zone_id}/assets` — assets currently in a zone.
AssetInZoneSummary.title: AssetInZoneSummary

AssetLoadRequest.description: POST /assets/{id}/load — attach a child asset to a carrier.
AssetLoadRequest.title: AssetLoadRequest

AssetPathPoint.description:
One point on an asset's merged movement path.

Sourced from either RFID tag reads (`source='rfid'`) or external position
fixes (`source` matches the originating ``external_locations.source``).
Returned in ascending chronological order.
AssetPathPoint.title: AssetPathPoint

AssetTagBindingCreate.description: Bind a tag value to an asset.
AssetTagBindingCreate.title: AssetTagBindingCreate

AssetUnloadRequest.description: POST /assets/{id}/unload — detach a child asset from its carrier.
AssetUnloadRequest.title: AssetUnloadRequest

DeviceCertAttach.description:
Admin payload for attaching a device certificate.

Plaintext PEM is **not** stored — only its SHA-256 thumbprint plus the
parsed subject. The MQTT broker (Mosquitto with EXTERNAL auth) holds the
PKI; the backend uses the thumbprint to map an authenticated cert back
to a device row.
DeviceCertAttach.title: DeviceCertAttach

DeviceTokenResponse.description: One-time device-token reveal — never re-readable after this response.
DeviceTokenResponse.title: DeviceTokenResponse

DeviceUpdate.description: Partial update for an existing device — all fields optional.
DeviceUpdate.title: DeviceUpdate

ExternalLocationCreate.description: Inbound non-RFID position fix for an asset.
ExternalLocationCreate.title: ExternalLocationCreate

Identity.description: Optional RFID identity payload (EPC / TID / user memory).
Identity.title: Identity

LatestTelemetryEntry.description:
Most-recent reading for a single metric on a subject.

Embedded on ``GET /assets/{id}`` and ``GET /lots/{id}`` (capped at
the 5 most-recently-written metrics per subject) so callers do not
have to issue a follow-up ``/telemetry/readings?...&limit=1`` for
each metric they want to display.
LatestTelemetryEntry.title: LatestTelemetryEntry

Location.description: Optional location attached to a tag read or sent on the location topic.
Location.title: Location

LoginRequest.description: Login with email and API key.
LoginRequest.title: LoginRequest

LoginResponse.description: Successful login response with JWT token.
LoginResponse.title: LoginResponse

LoginUserInfo.description: User info returned in login response.
LoginUserInfo.title: LoginUserInfo

ManifestEntry.description: One node in an asset's containment tree.
ManifestEntry.title: ManifestEntry

ManifestResponse.description: GET /assets/{id}/manifest — recursive children of a carrier.
ManifestResponse.title: ManifestResponse

MapConfigResponse.description: Resolved tile-provider config returned to the UI.

kind.description: Provider kind: osm | mapbox | maptiler | self_hosted
kind.title: Kind

MapConfigResponse.title: MapConfigResponse

MetricDefinition.description: A single metric that a device type can report.
MetricDefinition.title: MetricDefinition

ReadsPerHour.description: Aggregation: tag read count per hour bucket.
ReadsPerHour.title: ReadsPerHour

TagDataMappingUpdate.description: Partial update for a tag-data-mapping (Sprint 27 B4).
TagDataMappingUpdate.title: TagDataMappingUpdate

TagReadCreate.description: Incoming tag read event — used by both HTTP and MQTT ingestion paths.
TagReadCreate.title: TagReadCreate

TagReadResponse.description: Tag read event returned from the API.
TagReadResponse.title: TagReadResponse

TelemetryAggregateBucket.description:
One bucket from ``/telemetry/aggregates``.

Returned in chronological order. Backed by ``cagg_telemetry_1m`` or
``cagg_telemetry_1h`` depending on the requested ``bucket_seconds``;
falls back to a live ``time_bucket`` over ``telemetry_readings`` for
arbitrary intervals.
TelemetryAggregateBucket.title: TelemetryAggregateBucket

TelemetryBatch.description: Batched telemetry payload — HTTP and MQTT share this shape.
TelemetryBatch.title: TelemetryBatch

TelemetryModelCreate.description:
Define the telemetry schema for a subject (Sprint 14 → Sprint 18).

``device_type`` is required when ``subject_kind='device'`` (the
original Sprint 14 case) and must be omitted otherwise. The DB
enforces the same rule via ``ck_telemetry_models_device_type_required``.
TelemetryModelCreate.title: TelemetryModelCreate

TelemetryModelResponse.description: Telemetry model definition returned from the API.
TelemetryModelResponse.title: TelemetryModelResponse

TelemetryModelUpdate.description:
Sprint 28 G1: PATCH-style update for a telemetry model.

Only ``metrics`` is mutable. ``subject_kind`` and ``device_type`` define
the model's identity (the Sprint 18 unique constraint
``ix_telemetry_models_tenant_subject`` keys on these), so changing them
via PATCH would amount to creating a different row — callers should
DELETE + POST instead.
TelemetryModelUpdate.title: TelemetryModelUpdate

TelemetryQuarantineResponse.description:
A quarantined telemetry reading awaiting model fix-up or review.

Sprint 18 added optional ``subject_kind`` / ``subject_id`` fields:
legacy back-filled rows leave them ``None``; multi-subject ingest
(Sprint 19) populates them so reviewers can see *what* the failed
reading was meant to describe.
TelemetryQuarantineResponse.title: TelemetryQuarantineResponse

TelemetryReading.description: A single telemetry reading inside a batched payload.
TelemetryReading.title: TelemetryReading

TelemetryReadingIngest.description:
A single subject-scoped telemetry reading (HTTP / MQTT ingest).

Same shape as :class:`TelemetryReading` plus the resolved subject
fields the caller wants attribution against. ``device_id`` is the
optional reporting device for cross-reference (e.g. the gateway
that uplinked an external observation).
TelemetryReadingIngest.title: TelemetryReadingIngest

TelemetryReadingResponse.description:
A persisted ``telemetry_readings`` row (Sprint 18).

The subject-scoped successor to :class:`TelemetryResponse`. Carries
the resolved subject (kind + id), the reporting device when known,
and the source vocabulary defined in
:doc:`docs/design/rfid-tag-data-model` §D4.
TelemetryReadingResponse.title: TelemetryReadingResponse

TenantConfig.description: Read-only view of tenant-scoped feature flags.
TenantConfig.title: TenantConfig

TenantConfigUpdate.description:
Admin-only payload for toggling tenant feature flags.

All fields are optional; PATCH semantics — only fields explicitly
provided are written. Sprint 19 added ``telemetry_subject_kinds``;
Sprint 22 added ``rate_limit_overrides`` (per-tenant ceilings —
keys ∈ ``{ingest, read, write, admin}``, values are
requests-per-minute; pass ``{}`` to clear all overrides).
TenantConfigUpdate.title: TenantConfigUpdate

TileProviderUpdate.description:
Admin payload for setting the tenant's tile provider.

``provider`` is the raw blob persisted to ``tenants.tile_provider``;
schema is provider-specific (see ``services.map_config``).
Pass ``None`` to fall back to the system default (OSM public).
TileProviderUpdate.title: TileProviderUpdate

UniqueTagsPerWindow.description: Aggregation: unique tag count per time window.
UniqueTagsPerWindow.title: UniqueTagsPerWindow

UsageRecord.description: A single usage record for a tenant.
UsageRecord.title: UsageRecord

UsageSummary.description: Aggregated usage summary for a billing period.
UsageSummary.title: UsageSummary

UserCreate.description: Create a new user within a tenant.
UserCreate.title: UserCreate

ZoneCreate.description:
Create a zone.

Three kinds (mirrors the DB ``ck_zones_kind_payload`` CHECK):

* ``reader_bound`` — requires a non-empty ``fixed_reader_ids`` list.
* ``geofence`` — requires ``polygon_geojson`` (a GeoJSON ``Polygon``).
* ``virtual`` — admin-defined logical grouping (no readers, no polygon).
  Used for cross-cutting categories like ``Cold-chain``, ``FDA-controlled``,
  or ``Critical assets``. Must NOT carry ``fixed_reader_ids`` or
  ``polygon_geojson``.
ZoneCreate.title: ZoneCreate

post.description: Approve a pending device (admin only).

Attach a client certificate to a device (admin only).

Stores SHA-256 thumbprint + subject. The actual PEM lives in the MQTT
broker's CA store, never in the application database.
Decommission a device — sets status to 'decommissioned'.
Reject a pending device (admin only).

Rotate a device's Bearer token (admin only).

Plaintext token is returned **once** — backend stores only its SHA-256
hash, immediately invalidating any prior token. Audit-logged and metered
per ADR-011 Phase 1 / docs/design/edge-device-contract.md §5.
Self-register a device using a tenant provisioning key.

schema.title: Response Provision Device Devices Provision Post

get.description: Check provisioning status of a device.

Liveness probe — fast, no dependency checks.

Kept for backward compatibility (Sprint 22 A6 + earlier callers). The
SPA contract is the richer ``/health/live`` shape.
Detailed health — includes queue sizes and component stats.

Liveness probe — Sprint 25 A1 SPA contract.

Returns ``{"status": "alive", "version": "<sha>", "build_time": "<iso8601>"}``
in <50ms with no DB / MQTT / migration touches. ``Cache-Control: no-store``
is set explicitly so the SWA edge / browser back-cache never memoize the
body — a stale "alive" response would defeat the SPA's startup gate when
the api goes down. Build identity comes from ``Settings.build_version`` and
``Settings.build_time`` (Dockerfile-baked at image build time).
Readiness probe — checks DB, MQTT, and migration version.
List all integration targets.

post.description: Create an integration target (webhook, SSE, export).

get.description: SSE endpoint — streams real-time events filtered by tenant and event type.

List delivery history for an integration target.

post.description: Define the telemetry schema for a device type.

get.description:
Removed in Sprint 21 (ADR-015 §6).

The Sprint 19 301 redirect to ``/telemetry-models/device/{device_type}``
has been removed after one full retention cycle. Callers must address
the subject-scoped path directly. Returns 410 Gone with a Location-style
hint so any forgotten clients still get a clear migration message.

patch.description:
Sprint 28 G1: update a telemetry model's metrics list.

Only ``metrics`` is mutable. To change ``subject_kind`` or ``device_type``
delete the model and POST a new one — those columns key the Sprint 18
unique constraint and are part of the model's identity.

get.description:
Sprint 19 subject-scoped telemetry-model lookup.

For ``subject_kind='device'`` ``key`` is the device_type;
for non-device kinds the only model permitted per tenant is
addressed with any non-empty ``key`` (typically the same string as
``subject_kind`` so URLs remain self-describing).
Time-bucketed avg/min/max/count for a single subject + metric.

parameters.description: Bucket width in seconds. 60 / 3600 hit the continuous aggregates; other values are computed live via time_bucket() over the raw hypertable.
parameters.name: bucket_seconds

schema.description: Bucket width in seconds. 60 / 3600 hit the continuous aggregates; other values are computed live via time_bucket() over the raw hypertable.
schema.title: Bucket Seconds
Response List Telemetry Aggregates Telemetry Aggregates Get

get.description:
Subject-scoped telemetry query (Sprint 19).

Returns rows from the new ``telemetry_readings`` hypertable. The
legacy device-only ``GET /telemetry`` endpoint stays as-is for the
Sprint 14 contract; this endpoint is the multi-subject successor.

schema.title: Response List Telemetry Readings Telemetry Readings Get

post.description:
Direct subject-scoped telemetry write (admin/editor only).

For external systems publishing pre-resolved subject readings
(e.g. a TMS pushing per-asset GPS speed). Bypasses the tag-borne
fan-out path. Source defaults to ``"external"`` per the schema.
Each persisted row is published as ``Topic.TELEMETRY_RECORDED`` so
the Sprint 20 ``telemetry.threshold`` rule path fires here too.

schema.title: Response Ingest Telemetry Readings Telemetry Readings Ingest Post
